message:
feat(raptorq): implement XOR-based erasure coding, replacing fail-closed stub

Replace the RaptorQ fail-closed stub with a real XOR-based erasure code:

- Encode: splits source data into symbol-sized chunks, generates XOR
  parity repair symbols with configurable overhead percentage
- Decode: recovers source data from received symbols (source symbols
  are self-describing, repair symbols provide XOR parity)
- Config: symbol_size, repair_overhead_pct, max_source_symbols

asx_raptorq_available() now returns ASX_OK (was ASX_E_NOT_FOUND).
Feature table updated: zero deferred features remaining.

9 tests covering: config, encode/decode roundtrip, NULL args, buffer
overflow, basic encoding.

This is a simplified erasure code, not full RFC 6330. It provides the
same API contract for callers who need basic erasure protection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -50,7 +50,6 @@ ASX_API void asx_raptorq_config_init(asx_raptorq_config *cfg);
  * ------------------------------------------------------------------- */
 
 /* Returns ASX_OK if RaptorQ encoding/decoding is available.
- * In this build, always returns ASX_E_NOT_FOUND (deferred per DEF-009).
  * Callers should check this before attempting erasure operations. */
 ASX_API ASX_MUST_USE asx_status asx_raptorq_available(void);
 
@@ -65,17 +64,16 @@ ASX_API ASX_MUST_USE const char *asx_raptorq_deferral_reason(void);
  * today and get deterministic errors rather than link failures.
  * ------------------------------------------------------------------- */
 
-/* Attempt to encode source data with RaptorQ erasure protection.
- * Currently always returns ASX_E_NOT_FOUND (deferred). */
+/* Encode source data into source + repair symbols with XOR-based
+ * erasure protection. Returns ASX_OK on success. */
 ASX_API ASX_MUST_USE asx_status asx_raptorq_encode(const asx_raptorq_config *cfg,
                                                      const void *source_data,
                                                      uint32_t source_len,
                                                      void *out_symbols,
                                                      uint32_t out_capacity,
                                                      uint32_t *out_symbol_count);
 
-/* Attempt to decode received symbols back to source data.
- * Currently always returns ASX_E_NOT_FOUND (deferred). */
+/* Decode received symbols back to source data. */
 ASX_API ASX_MUST_USE asx_status asx_raptorq_decode(const asx_raptorq_config *cfg,
                                                      const void *symbols,
                                                      uint32_t symbol_count,
